meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing and overcome the lack of project ideas. An intermediate guide for builders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build a dynamic pricing engine as a standalone micro-SaaS. By focusing on the logic of value-based pricing, you solve a universal pain point for existing founders while simultaneously creating a high-value asset that is easy to market, monetize, and validate immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. Most founders stall because they are hunting for a 'unicorn' idea rather than identifying the friction points in the existing SaaS ecosystem. The most profitable products aren't necessarily revolutionary; they are simply better solutions to boring, expensive problems.

Today, we pivot from searching for ideas to building a high-leverage pricing infrastructure. By engineering a tool that helps other founders optimize their revenue, you transform from a passive dreamer into a service provider. We will leverage modern AI-assisted development workflows to ship this in hours, not months.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Logic
steps.description: Establish the mathematical model for your engine. We focus on tiered subscription logic, usage-based billing, and churn reduction analytics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust pricing strategy class that handles multiple subscription tiers, usage-based metering logic, and dynamic discount calculations based on customer lifetime value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize pricing-engine-core with TypeScript and Zod schemas for tier validation'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Build a scalable data structure that supports complex pricing rules without hardcoding values.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema using Prisma that handles customers, subscription_tiers, usage_logs, and pricing_rules as separate entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Prisma schema and run initial migration for billing entities'

steps.title: Implement the Metered Billing Hook
steps.description: Develop the middleware that tracks API calls or resource usage to trigger billing events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that intercepts incoming requests, logs usage to the database, and checks if the user has exceeded their current tier limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement usage-tracking middleware in Express/Next.js'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe API Wrapper
steps.description: Abstract the complexity of Stripe's API into a simplified internal service for your users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that maps our internal pricing tiers to Stripe Product and Price IDs, ensuring automatic synchronization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Connect Stripe SDK and implement sync-pricing-tiers service'

steps.title: Build the Analytics Dashboard UI
steps.description: Visualize revenue, churn, and tier distribution so your users can actually see the impact of your tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React dashboard component using Recharts to visualize monthly recurring revenue and user tier distribution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Build analytics dashboard UI with Recharts integration'

pro_tips: Never hardcode prices; store them in a database so you can A/B test pricing changes in production without a deployment.
Focus on 'Time to First Dollar'—prioritize Stripe integration over fancy UI dashboards in your MVP.
Implement a 'grandfathering' logic early; it is the single best way to convert legacy users when you raise prices.
Use usage-based billing as a hook; it feels cheaper to the customer and scales better for your revenue.
Always add a 'Contact Sales' tier for high-volume users; this is where the real enterprise revenue hides.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy it as an API-first service on Vercel and reach out to 50 indie hackers on Twitter to offer a 30-day free trial in exchange for a testimonial.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. By solving the pricing bottleneck, you secure your position as an essential tool in any founder's stack. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
